sutta: on 1 occasion the exalted 1 was dwelling near varanja at the foot of nimba tree there paharada the asura king came and visited the exalted 1 saluted him and stood at 1 side so standing the exalted 1 addressed him thus i imagine that the asuras find the light in the mighty ocean just stop here for a moment these asuras are devas heavenly beings and they used to reside in the tabatimsa heaven the heaven of the 33 by sakhadeva raja besides but it seems that saka deva raja threw the asuras out of the heaven down into the ocean 1 day when the asuras were drunk because the asuras they like to drink and they like to fight and so 1 day then they were quite drunk the devas the saka deva raja and his other devas threw these asuras down into the ocean and it seems that they reside there but once a year once a celestial year they'll go up to the tabatimsa heaven and fight with sakadeva raja and the other dev is 1 of the asura kings there are several asura kings another 1 another name that is often mentioned is veipachiti another 1 rahu is supposed to be 1 of the biggest divas so the asura king answered yes lord they find pleasure therein then the buddha said but how many wondrous marvels are there which the asuras delight to see and see lord there are 8 uh there are these 8 wondrous marvels 1 8 lord the mighty ocean slopes away gradually falls away gradually shells away gradually with no abruptness like a precipice lord that the mighty ocean slopes away gradually falls away gradually shelves away gradually with no abruptness like a precipice this is the first wondrous marvel which the asuras delight to see and see lord the ocean is fixed it does not overrun its bounds this is the second marvel lord the ocean does not consort with a dead body a corpse whatsoever a dead body there be in the ocean it will quickly just force ashore and parallel on the land this is the third marble lord all the great rivers the ganga the yamuna the achiravatis the sarabhu and the mahi on reaching the mighty ocean lose their former names and identities and are reckoned simply as the ocean this is the fourth marvel lord though all the streams in the world flow into the ocean and the rains that fall from the sky yet by then neither the emptiness nor the fullness of the ocean is affected this is the fifth marvel lord the mighty ocean has 1 taste the trees of salt this is the sixth marvel lord the mighty ocean has many and diverse treasures there is the pearl the crystal the lapis lazuli the shell quartz coral silver and gold ruby cat's eye etc this is the seventh marvel lord the mighty ocean is the home of vast beings there are the fabulous fishy monsters the chimneys there are in the mighty ocean creatures a 100 leagues long 3 2034 and 5 100 leagues long lord that the mighty ocean is the abode of vast beings timis to mingalas asuras nagas gandavas and creatures weeks long this is the eighth wondrous marvel which asuras delight to see and see these lord are the 8 wondrous marvels i suppose lord the monks find delight in this dharma yes paharada they do but lord how many wondrous marvels are there in this dhamma vinaya which the monks delight to see and see i just stopped here for a moment tamma vinaya is the teachings of the buddha in the sutas the discourses of the buddha the buddha always called his teachings the dhamma vinaya and in the angutra nikaya 4.180 the buddha said dhamma refers to the sutas that means the sutas and the vinaya books are the complete teachings of the buddha and the word tipitaka or tripitaka was only coined later it was not a word that was used by the buddha then the buddha continued there are 8 baharana just as the mighty ocean slopes away gradually falls away gradually shelves away gradually with no abruptness like a precipice even so in this dhammavinaya there is a graduated training a graduated practice a graduated mode of progress with no abruptness such as a penetration of knowledge that in this dhamma vinaya there is a graduated training a graduated practice a graduated mode of progress with no abruptness such as a penetration of noise of knowledge this is the first wondrous marvel in this dhamma vinaya which the monks delight to see and see [Music] this as the ocean is fixed and does not overpass its bounds even so when the code of training is made known by me to my disciples they will not transgress it even for life's sake this is the second marvel just as the ocean does not consort with a dead body a corpse but will quickly just force it ashore and pile it on the land even so whosoever is wicked of evil nature unclean of suspicious conduct full of secret actions no recruits though vowed thereto unchaste no vow to chastity rotten to the core lustful and vile not with him will the sangha consort but quickly assembling it will cast him forth though he be seated in the midst of the assembled monks yet is he far from the sangha and the sangha is far from him this is the third marvel just as the great rivers the ganga the yamuna the achiravati the salabu and the mahi entering the mighty ocean lose their former names and identities and a term simply ocean even so these 4 cars the katyas the brahmins the vesas and sudas going forth from the world into the homeless life into the dhamma vinaya proclaimed by the tata gata lose their former names and lineages and i reckon simply recluses sons of the sakyan this is the fourth marvel this is all the streams that flow into the ocean all the rains that fall from the sky affect neither the emptiness nor the fullness of the ocean even so though many monks become completely cool in the cool element to which nothing attaches here neither the emptiness nor the fullness of that cool element is affected this is the fifth marvel just as the ocean has but 1 taste the taste of salt even so this dhamma vinaya has but 1 flavor the flavor of liberation this is the sixth marvel pahrada just as the ocean has many and diverse treasures the pearl the crystal and so forth even so this dhamma vinaya has many and diverse treasures that is to say the 4 intense states of mindfulness sati patana the 4 right efforts sama padana the 4 basis of psychic power or 4 basis of success the 5 faculties indriya the 5 powers bala the 7 factors of enlightenment bojanga and the aryan eightfold path this is the seventh marvel paharada just as the mighty ocean is the home of vast beings the timi the timing gala and so forth even so this dhamma vinaya is the home of great beings that is to say the stream winner and who he who has entered the path to the realization of the fruit of the stream winner the once returner and he who has entered the path to the realization of the fruit thereof the non-returner and he who has entered the path to the realization of the fruit thereof the arahant and he who has entered the path to our handship paharada that this dhammavinaya is the home of great beings the stream winner and so forth this is the eighth wondrous marvel which the monks delight to see and see verily these are the 8 wondrous marvels which the monks delight to see and see that's the end of the suta
commentary:
it's 1 of those quite delightful sutas to hear this has the there are 8 wonderful qualities about the ocean so the buddha says there are 8 wonderful qualities about the teachings the first 1 is that in the dharma there is a gradual training a gradual practice a gradual mode of progress and here the buddha says with no abruptness such as a penetration of knowledge this is about the only place that is quite clear here that the buddha says there is no abruptness as such as a penetration of knowledge no abruptness so this translation is quite accurate that means you practice the holy part and the the spiritual path and you don't expect to suddenly have an attainment of knowledge it's a very gradual process and so that's why we should not be too [Music] um too eager to get results it takes time uh we we need to just put the effort in the spiritual path and the slowly yeah we will attain the insights in time to come very gradually there is no abruptness of attainment of knowledge and then the second 1 um the buddha said now that his disciples will not transgress his court of training the precepts etc even for life's sake and the third 1 the sangha will not consort with a wicked evil monk but they will throw him out of the sanghas out of the out of the sangha they will meet and throw him out if they they will expel him if they know that he's a false insincere monk and then the fourth 1 not just as there are 4 uh just just as all the great rivers enter the ocean and lose their former names so in india at that time there were 4 castles the katyas are the warrior cast the brahmins are the priests cars the veisas are the merchants and the sudas are the labour castle but when they go forth and become a manga they lose all their castle they are just called samanas recluses sons of the sakya sakyaputa [Music] but even though they lose their cars some of them are they are still call by their names and their surnames for example kasapa kasapa is a surname is also a surname but sometimes they have their personal names like for example sarikuta sarikuta means the son of sari so the fifth wonderful quality about the dhamma vinaya is that though many monks become completely cool yet the emptiness or the fullness of nibana is not affected because nibana is is not a place it is a statement that is beyond time and beyond space it is not in this world that is why the emptiness or fullness of nirvana is not affected by the number of monks who enter nibana the sixth quality here is that the teachings of the buddha the dhamma vinaya has only 1 flavor the flavor of liberation from suffering and that is the purpose of the spiritual path to attain depression from suffering and the 7 qualities just as the ocean has many treasures so the buddha says in the dharmavinaya there are these various treasures which actually are the 37 bodhipakya dharmas the requisites of enlightenment the 4 sati patana the 4 sama padana the 4 idi pada 5 india 5 bala 7 bojanga and the noble eightfold path then um eighth 1 just as the ocean is the home of many beings so the dhamma vinaya is the home of great beings who are the aryans aryans those the stream winner and the 1 was entered the path to stream winning the once return and 1 was entered the path to 1's returning etc actually in this translation we know that the translation reads like this this the original translation in the book the stream winner and he who attains to the realization of the fruit of stream winner this when i check the pali i feel is wrong because according to their translation it means that the stream winner is a path attainer but actually the to my interpretation the stream winner is the fruit the fruit attainer nepali is [Music] or realization of the fruit of stream entry so 1 who has entered the path for the realization of the fruit of stream entry and that is the path attainer not the food attainers or the sota panna must be the fruit attain these are the so each of these terms has they refer to the fruit attainer and you note here there are 8 individuals not 4 individuals the aryans are always described as 8 not 4 and in other sutas we will see that the path attainer and the food attainer they are different persons it's not that 1 moment it attains the path and the next moment he attains the fruit it's not like that in the sutras in the the the after attaining the path it might take years before they attain the fruit so these are the all the 8 wonderful qualities of the dhammavinaya
